title: Naivedya (Food Offerings)
summary: How to prepare and offer food to the deity and receive it back as prasad. Applies in both home and temple worship; specifics vary by sect.

steps.title: Prepare food with cleanliness
steps.instruction: Naivedya is prepared in a clean kitchen by someone bathed and in clean clothes. Do not taste the food while cooking — the first portion goes to the deity untouched.

steps.title: Keep it satvik
steps.instruction: Common offerings: cooked rice, sweets (kheer, laddu), fruits, milk, tulsi leaves (Vaishnava), bel leaves (Shaiva), bananas, coconut, jaggery. Avoid onion, garlic, meat, eggs, and alcohol in naivedya.

steps.title: Place the food on the altar
steps.instruction: Set the offering plate directly in front of the deity. Sprinkle a few drops of water around the plate (parishechana) to demarcate it as an offering.

steps.title: Offer with mantra
steps.instruction: Hold the offering with folded hands and offer it mentally to the deity. A common verse across traditions is the 'brahmārpaṇaṁ' verse from the Gita.

mantras.text: ॐ ब्रह्मार्पणं ब्रह्म हविर्ब्रह्माग्नौ ब्रह्मणा हुतम्।
mantras.transliteration: oṃ brahmārpaṇaṁ brahma havir brahmāgnau brahmaṇā hutam
mantras.meaning: Brahman is the offering, Brahman the offered, offered by Brahman into the fire of Brahman

steps.title: Wait and receive as prasad
steps.instruction: Wait a short interval (commonly a few minutes — the time it takes for the deity to accept). Then partake of the food as prasad. Share with family. Do not throw prasad away; treat leftovers with respect.

variants.label: Vaishnava households
variants.instruction: Tulsi leaves are placed on top of the offering and considered essential. Food not offered first is not eaten.

sources.quote: A leaf, a flower, a fruit, water — whoever offers to me with devotion, that offering of the pure-hearted I accept.
Brahman is the offering, Brahman the oblation, poured by Brahman into the fire of Brahman...
